content:
on the discrimination case. And I think the discrimination case is probably, if I had, if you put a gun to my head and ask me, you know, what do I actually think? I would say I think that's the one we lose on.

You
Wow, okay.

Participant
And but I think we win on the retaliation side of it because I think that you provided enough. You know, you're a big enough pain in their ass because you were exercising infected, right? That they just figured, fuck it. let's just not renew this contract. And another mistake they're making is they think that non-renewal of a contract, is a, is a, uh, is a, uh, uh, um, is, is it a protected activity, and it actually is, anything that you do that would dissuade a reasonable person from, from exercising the protected rights can, can be an adverse action. In other words, their position is is, oh, we didn't fire him. We just did renew his contract.

You
Mm.

Participant
It just expired nationally. Okay? In reality is, is that we're able to allege that, you didn't get the contract renewal because of, your complaints and and requests for accommodation and your exercising those protected rights.
